title_en: Tzaar Baalei Chaim: The Torah's Animal Welfare Laws
title_he: צער בעלי חיים: דיני התורה לרווחת בעלי חיים
title_zh: 动物福利：犹太教的动物待遇法律
cover_image_description: A serene Chinese brushstroke landscape in shan shui style showing a shepherd gently tending to animals in a peaceful valley. In the foreground, a man helps an overburdened donkey while nearby sheep graze. Rolling hills, sparse pine trees, traditional ink wash technique in muted earth tones. Mood: contemplative, peaceful, emphasizing kindness to creatures.

bibliography.title: Deuteronomy 22:6-7 - The Law of Sending Away the Mother Bird
Shabbat 128b - Talmudic Discussion on Animal Suffering
Guide for the Perplexed, Part 3, Chapter 48
Kosher Slaughter: An Introduction to Shechita
Kindness to Animals: Tzaar Baalei Chaim
Rabbi Samson Raphael Hirsch on Animals

summary_en: Explores the Torah's approach to animal welfare (*tzaar baalei chaim*): biblical roots, Talmudic development, halachic applications (from pet ownership to factory farming), and its impact on character, with practical guidelines.
summary_he: סקירה על יחס התורה לבעלי חיים — שורשים בתורה, עיצוב הדינים בתלמוד, דוגמאות ויישומים מעשיים וחינוך לחמלה, כולל הנחיות מעשיות.
summary_zh: 探讨犹太教动物福利律法的来源、发展及其对现代生活的指导意义，包括实际守则。
